title: Consolidate Input Handler Classes
description: The input handling layer has five overlapping classes (TradeKeyGuy, TradeMouseTrap, KeyGuy, MouseTrap, Scene2DMousePanTrap) with duplicated patterns for key/mouse event routing, camera binding, and scene-specific overrides. Consolidate these into a unified input abstraction that scenes can configure declaratively, reducing the surface area for input bugs and making new scene creation simpler.
definition-of-done: 1. A single composable input handler abstraction replaces the five separate classes. 2. Existing scenes (TradeScene, IrregularGridScene, LineScene, etc.) work identically with the new input system. 3. Camera pan/zoom behavior from Scene2DMousePanTrap is available as a reusable component. 4. Scene-specific key bindings (TradeKeyGuy extras) are expressible via configuration or composition, not separate classes. 5. MouseTrap static scroll-region subscription API is preserved or replaced with equivalent. 6. AutomationInputBinder still works for test automation.
testing-plan: 1. Verify TradeScene camera pan, zoom, and key shortcuts work identically. 2. Verify IrregularGridScene pan/zoom with Scene2DMousePanTrap behavior preserved. 3. Verify scroll-region subscriptions still fire correctly. 4. Verify automation input binding works through the new abstraction. 5. Regression test all scenes that currently use any of the five classes.
todos: Audit all five classes and catalog their shared vs unique responsibilities
Design a composable InputHandler interface with camera-bind, key-route, and mouse-route capabilities
Implement unified handler with configuration for scene-specific overrides
Migrate TradeScene to the new input handler
Remove the five legacy classes once all scenes are migrated
unknowns: Whether composition (decorating a base handler) or configuration (flags/options on a single handler) is the better model
Whether MouseTrap static state (scroll region subscriptions) needs to become instance-scoped
How deeply TradeKeyGuy key bindings are coupled to trade-specific game state
